arts.ttl: Ranieri: “I Will Leave Roma at the End of the Season, They Are Looking For a New Coach”
arts.sum: As the Serie A season progresses, Roma's coach Claudio Ranieri has made headlines with his recent statements regarding his future and the club's search for a new manager. Ranieri, who has been brought back to stabilize the team after a disappointing start under previous management, will be a guest on the Rai Due show "Dribbling" on January 18. The post Ranieri: “I Will Leave Roma at the End of the Season, They Are Looking For a New Coach” appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Mario Balotelli’s Time at Genoa Coming to an End: A Possible Return to Monza
arts.sum: Mario Balotelli’s brief stint with Genoa appears to be nearing its conclusion, just over two months after he joined the club. Initially signed under the guidance of former coach Alberto Gilardino, who advocated for his arrival, Balotelli's time at Genoa has been marked by limited playing opportunities and a lack of alignment with the current coaching staff led by Patrick Vieira. The post Mario Balotelli’s Time at Genoa Coming to an End: A Possible Return to Monza appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Pellegrini Comments on Criticism and His Roma Future
arts.sum: Lorenzo Pellegrini had a liberating goal in the recent derby with Lazio, but his relationship with the Roma faithful remains checkered. He addresses the rumors, as he has been blamed for conspiring to get José Mourinho and Daniele De Rossi fired. The post Pellegrini Comments on Criticism and His Roma Future appeared first on The Cult of Calcio.

arts.ttl: Pellegrini: “Leaving Roma? The Pitch Decides. I Did Not Betray Mourinho and De Rossi”
arts.sum: Roma captain Lorenzo Pellegrini has given a candid interview to Corriere dello Sport, addressing recent controversies, his commitment to the club, and his thoughts on the future. The midfielder spoke openly about the shock of Jose Mourinho's dismissal, his relationship with current manager Claudio Ranieri, and rumours surrounding his own future at the club. The post Pellegrini: “Leaving Roma? The Pitch Decides. I Did Not Betray Mourinho and De Rossi” appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Serie A | Roma v Genoa: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: As Roma prepare to face Genoa in a crucial Serie A fixture, manager Claudio Ranieri finds himself in an enviable position with an almost fully fit squad at his disposal. The only notable absentee for Friday's encounter is Bryan Cristante, who is in the final stages of recovery from an ankle injury. The post Serie A | Roma v Genoa: Probable starting lineups appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Roma vs Genoa to open matchday 21
arts.sum: Roma have kept four clean sheets in their last six Serie A matches against Genoa, as many as in their previous 14 games against them; furthermore, the Giallorossi have only failed to score once in their last 19 games against the Rossoblu in the competition. Genoa have won 33 matches against Roma in Serie A (D26 L54), only winning more against Lazio (38 wins); however, the Rossoblu have collected only one win in their last 19 matches against the Giallorossi in the competition (D55 L13).
